input.prompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

input.initialPrompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

data.content:
Here's a complete example:

**XAML:**
```xml
<CollectionView
    ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
    SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem, Mode=TwoWay}"
    SelectionMode="Single"
    IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}"
    RemainingItemsThreshold="0">

    <CollectionView.Header>
        <RefreshView
            Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
            IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">
        </RefreshView>
    </CollectionView.Header>

    <!-- Wrap the whole CollectionView in a RefreshView instead: -->
</CollectionView>
```

The standard pattern is to **wrap** the `CollectionView` in a `RefreshView`:

```xml
<RefreshView
    Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
    IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">

    <CollectionView
        ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
        SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem, Mode=TwoWay}"
        SelectionMode="Single">

        <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
            <DataTemplate x:DataType="models:Item">
                <Grid Padding="12" ColumnDefinitions="*,Auto">
                    <Label Text="{Binding Name}" FontSize="16" />
                    <Label Grid.Column="1" Text="›" />
                </Grid>
            </DataTemplate>
        </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
    </CollectionView>
</RefreshView>
```

**ViewModel:**
```csharp
public partial class ItemsViewModel : ObservableObject
{
    private readonly IApiService _api;
    private readonly INavigationService _nav;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private ObservableCollection<Item> _items = new();

    [ObservableProperty]
    private bool _isRefreshing;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private Item? _selectedItem;

    // Triggered when SelectedItem changes (source-generated)
    partial void OnSelectedItemChanged(Item? value)
    {
        if (value is not null)
        {
            _nav.NavigateAsync("DetailPage", new { Id = value.Id });
            SelectedItem = null; // clear selection highlight
        }
    }

    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task RefreshAsync()
    {
        try
        {
            var data = await _api.GetItemsAsync();
            Items = new ObservableCollection<Item>(data);
        }
        finally
        {
            IsRefreshing = false; // stops the spinner
        }
    }
}
```

**Key bindings explained:**

| Binding | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"` | Drives the list from an `ObservableCollection` |
| `SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem, Mode=TwoWay}"` | Two-way so the VM sees taps **and** can clear selection |
| `SelectionMode="Single"` | Only one item selectable at a time |
| `Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"` | `RefreshView` calls `RefreshAsync` on pull-down |
| `IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}"` | Controls the refresh spinner; set `false` when done |

**Important:** Set `SelectedItem = null` after navigating — otherwise the same item can't be tapped twice in a row, and it stays visually highlighted when the user returns.
